steps.title: Find the contrasting claim
steps.content: Text 1 states that poets can write between shifts, in the hospital pantry, on the subway, and on scraps of surplus paper, showing that worthwhile poetry can be made while working other jobs.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content: Choice B captures Lorde’s point that poetry can be valuable even when written outside the professional, full‑time‑job context described in Text 2. The other three either misstate Lorde’s view, introduce unrelated ideas, or reverse the relationship. That matches choice B — B is correct.
